observations.summary: The physical and emotional tension described in the source paragraph parallels earlier descriptions of Dan's somatic response to spiritual and psychological conflict, emphasizing the visceral cost of his defiance and willpower.

source_evidence.quote: He was not merely terrified. He was shattered. The muscles clenched to rigid, jaw locked, breathing a frantic high-pitched whistle. He had poured his will into shaping his father's dream and then spent every reserve defying something that had been old when Hebron was new.

observations.target_hint: Earlier chapters describe Dan's physical and emotional struggle as he confronts internal and external conflicts, highlighting the recurring motif of bodily cost accompanying spiritual defiance.

target_evidence.quote: He was not merely shaken; the cost of his defiance instantly registered as a rigid, painful knot in his muscles. His jaw felt tight. His breathing was shallow, a frantic, high-pitched whistle.
